should_trigger: We have twelve agent tasks and the plan keeps causing rework because later research changes earlier architecture decisions. Use a matrix method to redesign the orchestration.
Help me sequence these product, backend, frontend, and data migration workstreams. Some can run in parallel, but I need to expose hidden dependencies and feedback loops.
Our auto-orchestrator has agents for retrieval, planning, coding, review, and evaluation. Map the dependencies and propose clusters or handoff contracts.
This workflow looks like a checklist, but every step depends on every other step. Find a better structure and identify bounded iteration loops.
Create a DSM for these API, database, model-eval, and deployment tasks, then identify which dependencies create iteration cycles.
Our plan has ten components and handoffs are repeatedly bouncing between teams. Use DSM to cluster work and reduce coordination cost.
should_not_trigger: Pick the highest priority feature from this list using impact versus effort scoring.
Summarize what Design Structure Matrix means in one paragraph for a glossary.
Build a kanban board for these tasks without analyzing dependency direction or iteration loops.
Choose a color palette for a dependency matrix visualization.
edge_cases: There are only four tasks, but two of them keep changing each other's requirements. Decide whether DSM is worth using or whether a simple loop contract is enough.
I do not yet know all the tasks in this project. Help me decide what discovery pass is needed before building a dependency matrix.
The dependencies are known, but the team only needs a one-time ordering and no iteration risk analysis; decide whether DSM is overkill.
The user mixes components, people, and risks in one matrix; decide how to normalize the element type before analysis.

compliance_expectations.runtime_dependency: Must answer from SKILL.md content only; do not require external source files, webpages, source reports, original books, external source folder, or distilled source text.
compliance_expectations.activation_gate: Should trigger only when hidden dependencies, feedback loops, sequencing uncertainty, clustering, parallelization, or coordination cost are central.
compliance_expectations.expected_behavior: Identify matrix boundary, element type, dependency direction, and decision objective, then produce elements, dependency map, diagnosis, transformation, iteration policy, and early validation priorities.
compliance_expectations.negative_behavior: Must not use DSM as priority scoring, mix incompatible element types without defining cell meaning, change dependency convention mid-analysis, or linearize legitimate iteration.
